title: Feed Discovery, WHATWG URL Identity, Redirects, and SSRF · Type-System Trace: Feed Ts Discovery Audit

Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Predict Feed Ts Discovery Audit · Theory Feed Ts Discovery Audit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 5601 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted, parsed, resolved, redirected, selected, and rejected URL evidence with tenant, actor, policy, and bounded causes.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Discovery Audit model · Theory Feed Ts Discovery…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The stored final URL fully explains source admission.
